methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $1.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Space & Aerospace. Range: $274M - $1.3B.

methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 45.3x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Stoke Space is a Series B space & aerospace company founded by Andy Lapsa and Tom Feldman. The target carries a current post-money valuation of $1.0B against $175M+ in total capital raised. The valuation assessment draws on a comparative universe of 3 same-stage private peers (Aalyria at $1.3B, Hubble Network at $400M, Sift at $274M) and 5 public space & aerospace comps (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) trading at a median 45.3x price-to-sales.

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical for Series B advancement to the $1.0B base, yielding $1.9B. Peer-median analysis across the 3 same-sector, same-stage private comparables produces $400M, reflecting the wide dispersion across that small cohort ($274M to $1.3B). Public-comp anchoring is inapplicable due to lack of target revenue disclosure. 

The two applicable methods triangulate to a range of $340M to $2.2B, with a median of $1.1B. This positioning near the current valuation reflects the tension between stage-typical progression ($1.9B) and the lower median of observable peer transactions ($400M). Key uncertainties include the target's revenue trajectory relative to peers, the relevance of the wide peer valuation dispersion, and materiality of unrealized technical or regulatory milestones specific to reusable launch systems.
